mechanics.name: Earthbend
mechanics.summary: Pay an earthbend cost to turn a land you control into a 0/0 haste creature with that many +1/+1 counters; it returns tapped when it dies. Green's ramp-into-beatdown and a counters payoff.

mechanics.name: Waterbend
mechanics.summary: An alternative cost you may pay by tapping untapped artifacts and creatures you control, each covering {1}. Rewards a wide board and cheap permanents — blue's tap-for-value engine.

mechanics.name: Firebending
mechanics.summary: Whenever this creature attacks, add that much red mana until end of combat. An aggressive ritual that bankrolls combat tricks, extra spells, and activated abilities mid-attack.

mechanics.name: Airbend
mechanics.summary: Exile a nonland permanent; its owner may recast it for {2}. A tempo/blink tool that re-triggers your own enters-the-battlefield effects or resets a threat, and feeds cast-from-exile payoffs.

mechanics.name: Lessons
mechanics.summary: Instants and sorceries with the Lesson subtype; several payoffs scale with or switch on when three-plus Lessons sit in your graveyard (Combustion Technique, Gran-Gran, The Lion-Turtle, Bumi), a cheap-spell value engine in blue/red/green.

mechanics.name: Ally typal
mechanics.summary: The set's dominant creature type (~31 cards). Go wide and lean on 'whenever an Ally enters or attacks' triggers, anthems, and token makers to convert a crowded board into damage and value.

mechanics.name: Avatar Sagas
mechanics.summary: 'The Legend of X // Avatar X' double-faced Sagas advance three chapters of value, then transform into a legendary Avatar bomb — the format's premium rares and mythics.

archetypes.name: Airbending fliers
archetypes.playstyle: Deploy evasive Allies, then lean on airbend tempo (Aang, Airbending Lesson, Glider Staff) to reset blockers and untangle attacks that draw you extra cards off Sokka and Teo. Air Nomad Legacy and Momo turn a modest air force into lethal while blue bombs and card advantage bury any ground stall.
keyCards: The Legend of Yangchen // Avatar Yangchen
Aang, Swift Savior // Aang and La, Ocean's Fury
The Legend of Kuruk // Avatar Kuruk

archetypes.name: Sacrifice grind
archetypes.playstyle: Manufacture expendable bodies — Ally tokens, Clues, dying Vehicles — then cash them in with Tolls of War, Suki, and Phoenix Fleet Airship for incremental value and counters. Best-in-class W/B removal keeps the board flat while you grind out the long game on attrition, not tempo.
keyCards: The Rise of Sozin // Fire Lord Sozin
The Legend of Yangchen // Avatar Yangchen

archetypes.name: Ally aggro
archetypes.playstyle: Flood the board with cheap Allies and 1/1 tokens, then convert width into damage with anthems and "creatures-you-control" scalers like United Front and the signposts; efficient red removal clears blockers so the swarm closes before opponents stabilize.
keyCards: The Legend of Yangchen // Avatar Yangchen
The Legend of Roku // Avatar Roku

archetypes.name: Ally go-wide
archetypes.playstyle: Flood the board with cheap Allies, then stack anthems and +1/+1 counters until United Front or a Suki swing turns the wide board lethal. Every Ally-enters trigger (life, draw, counters) compounds, and the fixing lets a bomb slot in over the top.
keyCards: The Legend of Yangchen // Avatar Yangchen
The Legend of Kyoshi // Avatar Kyoshi

archetypes.name: Draw-two control
archetypes.playstyle: Chain Clue tokens to reliably hit your second card each turn, spinning up evasive Spirit tokens and +1/+1 counters while premium black removal and board wipes stall to your bombs. Clue density is the real engine — this is a grindy card-advantage control deck, not a tempo one.
keyCards: The Rise of Sozin // Fire Lord Sozin
The Legend of Kuruk // Avatar Kuruk

archetypes.name: Lessons tempo
archetypes.playstyle: Chain cheap Lessons to stock the graveyard and feed count-scaling payoffs (Combustion Technique, Ran and Shaw) plus noncreature-spell triggers, while burn and blue card advantage keep the tempo lead. Most of the burn is creature-only, so bombs and evasive beaters do the closing.
keyCards: The Legend of Kuruk // Avatar Kuruk
The Legend of Roku // Avatar Roku

archetypes.name: Lessons ramp
archetypes.playstyle: The set's greediest deck: chain green ramp and self-mill through the early turns, then bury them with blue Lessons value and X-spell bombs. Bank three Lessons in your graveyard to switch on Lion-Turtle, Gran-Gran, and Bumi.
payoffs: The Legend of Kyoshi // Avatar Kyoshi
keyCards: The Legend of Kuruk // Avatar Kuruk

archetypes.name: Firebending aggro
archetypes.playstyle: Curve out with cheap firebending bodies and Raid attackers, then swing every turn while red burn and black removal blast blockers out of the way. Attack triggers and sacrifice payoffs snowball the board; stay aggressive and prioritize efficient two-drops plus removal over top-end.
keyCards: The Legend of Roku // Avatar Roku
The Rise of Sozin // Fire Lord Sozin

archetypes.name: Earthbend counters
archetypes.playstyle: Grind a +1/+1 counter engine: turn lands into creatures with earthbend, pile counters, and recycle dying bodies into more counters via Beifong's Bounty Hunters and Long Feng. Out-size the board with green fatties while black's deep removal clears the path.
keyCards: The Rise of Sozin // Fire Lord Sozin
The Legend of Kyoshi // Avatar Kyoshi

archetypes.name: Earthbend stompy
archetypes.playstyle: Curve out on power-4 bodies and earthbend your lands into counters, then turn everything sideways while "four-power matters" rewards snowball board and cards. Red removal clears blockers so the big green threats connect.
keyCards: The Legend of Roku // Avatar Roku
The Legend of Kyoshi // Avatar Kyoshi

combos.note: The Clue's extra draw each turn trips Foggy Swamp's second-draw trigger for a Spirit token
Cracking the Clue hits your second draw and grows Knowledge Seeker
Messenger Hawk's Clue and second-draw bonus both feed the draw-two engine
Momo discounts and pumps your fliers while Teo loots whenever they attack
United Front's token flood spikes Suki's power and each attack adds another attacker
The anthem turns United Front's 1/1 Allies into a real board
Every Ally token United Front makes stacks a counter on Avatar Enthusiasts
A wide board keeps Sokka's attack-draw online every combat
Every land the two earthbend piles counters that set Toph's power
Dai Li's double earthbend triggers Ascension's landfall and grows the counter-creatures its attack drains from
Each Lesson in the yard grows Combustion Technique and switches on Gran-Gran's discount
Casting Lessons like Combustion Technique fills the yard so Ran and Shaw copies itself on entry
Herbalist pays for Lessons that fill the yard and free the Lion-Turtle to swing
Every Lesson Herbalist casts widens Bumi's enters-the-battlefield choices
Recasting the airbent permanent triggers Appa's cast-from-exile Ally token
Hei Bai's attack sacrifice feeds a Tolls of War Ally token and grows Hei Bai
Sacrificing a Clue each turn spawns a Tolls Ally and stacks Obsessive Pursuit's attack counters
Stacked firebending attack-mana keeps Ozai above the six-mana line for flying and indestructible
A board full of United Front Allies turns Sun Warriors' attack into a mountain of red mana
A wide Ally board turns Jet's entry into a huge burst of damage
Appa's attack airbend re-blinks Jailer for a {2} recast, re-exiling the best enemy permanent each turn
the Ally token flood taps to pay Katara's waterbend {X}, turning every 1/1 into an X/X
tapping Gran-Gran to pay Katara's waterbend {6} draw bolts a free loot onto every activation
Hawk's Clue cracks for the second draw each turn that triggers Raven Eagle's drain
the loot Lesson stocks the graveyard with Lessons that scale Combustion Technique's exile-burn
one cheap Lesson in the graveyard permanently switches on Cadets' firebending 2
the trick makes a Clue off the Mechanist's trigger that he then animates into a 3/1 flier
each cheap Lesson cast drops a Pathik counter and fuels Wisdom's three-Lesson draw-three upgrade
every June sacrifice-into-Clue (or Clue crack) on your turn also nets an Ally token from Tolls
sacrificing Poxbearers to Hei Bai banks two +1/+1 counters while the body replaces itself with an Ally token
Warriors' two Ally arrivals (body plus token) put two counters on the Lieutenant
Freedom Fighters plus its Ally token trigger Protestors twice for instant hasty counters
cracking Azula's attack Clue with her own firebending mana triggers Zhao's team-wide +1/+0 mid-combat
sacrificing a creature to pay Precision has Beifong's earthbend for that creature's full power
the death-trigger earthbend 4 puts four land counters that directly grow Toph's power
the land sacrificed to Cycle triggers Long Feng, converting double ramp into a free +1/+1 counter
the instant 4/4 Bear turns on Bitter Work's draw-per-attack with a power-4 creature
casting the Lesson earthbends with a bonus counter via Toph on top of the Lesson's own earthbend 4
